meaning: n.捏造者,制作者,假造者,杜撰者
examples: A basic 3D printer, also known as a fabricator or “fabber”, now costs less than a laser printer did in 1985.
There will be no need for factories, goes the logic, when every village has a fabricator that can produce items when needed.
CIMC has been the world's largest container fabricator since 1996 and its market share now? Accounts for 55% of the world? Total.
Lutz also rejected Cunningham's and Black's choice of a fabricator to build the concept car.
In addition, they quoted additional evidence from people they had been told was a fabricator.
